title: WAVE-P: Hardware Acceleration for the APV Professional Video Codec - Semiwiki
gist: Chips&Media launched WAVE-P, a hardware IP core for the intra-frame APV professional video codec, claiming a single core handles 8K30 4:4:4 12-bit encode/decode at 500MHz for about 14.6-14.7 mW, with APV itself delivering a 19.3% average BD-rate improvement over a conventional professional codec, aimed at bringing broadcast-grade video into cameras, mobile devices, and edge production hardware.

themes.theme: APV codec efficiency and WAVE-P hardware performance

insights.claim: The Advanced Professional Video (APV) codec is an intra-frame codec that delivers an average 19.3 percent BD-rate improvement over a conventional professional video codec in tested 2K and 4K 4:2:2 sequences, while supporting chroma formats from 4:2:2 to 4:4:4, bit depths from 10 to 16 bits, HDR10/HDR10+, and multiview content.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A quantified 19.3% BD-rate (compression efficiency) improvement over existing professional codecs is a concrete competitive data point for evaluating APV against alternatives like ProRes or DNxHD in professional video workflows.
insights.quote: APV delivers an average 19.3 percent BD-rate improvement over a conventional professional video codec in tested 2K and 4K 4:2:2 sequences.

insights.claim: Chips&Media's WAVE-P hardware IP core is specified to handle 8K video at 30 frames per second at 500 MHz in a 4:4:4 12-bit profile, with simulated performance of about 1.07 gigapixels per second encoding and 1.05 gigapixels per second decoding, at estimated power of approximately 14.7 mW encoding and 14.6 mW decoding under 7nm test conditions.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Sub-15mW power for 8K30 4:4:4 12-bit professional video encode/decode is a specific, checkable performance/power benchmark for evaluating WAVE-P against competing video codec IP for battery-powered cameras and mobile devices doing professional-grade capture.
insights.quote: Power estimates are approximately 14.7 mW for encoding and 14.6 mW for decoding under the stated 7 nm test conditions.

insights.claim: The full WAVE-P codec configuration is estimated at 657,000 logic gates plus 12.93 KB of memory, for a total equivalent area of 887,000 gates, and the architecture supports multi-core scalability and tile-based parallel processing, allowing throughput to scale without redesigning the entire pipeline.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A specific gate-count/area footprint (887,000 gates) is a concrete silicon-cost input for SoC designers evaluating whether to integrate WAVE-P into camera, mobile, or media-processing chips versus competing codec IP or software-only approaches.
insights.quote: The full codec configuration is estimated at 657,000 logic gates plus 12.93 KB of memory, for a total equivalent area of 887,000 gates.
